title: Self-improvement P1/P2 (offline, gated): replay harness, evaluator anti-degeneration guards, GEPA prompt evolution
description: Harden self-improvement so it is safe to turn on. Add a frozen replay regression harness (the gate), three off-by-default evaluator anti-degeneration guards, and an offline replay-gated GEPA prompt-evolution CLI. The cross-cutting invariant: the system never edits its own prompts/lessons without a deterministic, replay-gated check, and live pipeline behavior is byte-identical until a new selfImprove flag is explicitly enabled.

clarificationQuestions.question: Where does the replay corpus live and what stores the baseline index?

options.label: A
options.description: Files under .bober/replay/cases/*.json (immutable) + SQLite index replay.db, reusing the FactStore adapter pattern.

options.label: B
options.description: A single monolithic JSON manifest with all cases inline.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — principles.md mandates filesystem state, SQLite is already used (src/state/facts.ts:3 better-sqlite3), and standalone JSON cases stay git-diffable + immutable.

clarificationQuestions.question: How does the deterministic-first gate short-circuit the LLM judge without changing default behavior?

options.label: A
options.description: A pure guard after runEvaluation in runEvaluatorAgent that skips runAgentEvaluation when a required programmatic strategy failed AND config.selfImprove.deterministicGate is true; default false keeps both calls running.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — runEvaluation already exposes per-strategy passed+required (registry.ts:181-203); off-by-default preserves the existing programmaticEval.passed && agentResult.passed combine (evaluator-agent.ts:116).

clarificationQuestions.question: What does rubric isolation strip from the generator-bound handoff, and from where?

options.label: A
options.description: A pure redactRubric(handoff) removing successCriteria, evaluatorNotes, and verificationMethod from currentContract, applied at createHandoff in pipeline.ts only when config.selfImprove.rubricIsolation is true.

options.label: B
options.description: Strip the entire contract from the handoff.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — the handoff carries the full currentContract (context-handoff.ts:47) including the rubric; the generator needs title/description/definitionOfDone/generatorNotes but not the evaluator's scoring criteria. Off-by-default.

clarificationQuestions.question: What makes a FAIL detail uncited and what happens to it under the cite-artifact guard?

options.label: A
options.description: A FAIL EvalDetail with no file (optionally line) AND no failing-test/command-output substring in message is downgraded to passed:true severity:info when config.selfImprove.requireCitedArtifact is true, so an uncited FAIL cannot fail the sprint.

options.label: B
options.description: Reject the whole evaluation if any FAIL is uncited.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — EvalDetail.file/line are optional (eval-result.ts:14-15) and the judge prompt already asks for file:line evidence (evaluator-agent.ts:289); a pure downgrade is conservative and deterministic.

clarificationQuestions.question: Does GEPA ever mutate the live agent .md files or the live pipeline?

options.label: A
options.description: No. bober evolve reads agents/<role>.md, writes variants + the replay-gated winner only under .bober/evolve/<runId>/, never to agents/ and never invoked by runPipeline; promotion to agents/ is a manual human copy, out of scope here.

options.label: B
options.description: evolve writes the winning prompt directly back to agents/<role>.md.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — research §5 P2 + §7 risk #3 demand replay-gated, offline-only self-edits; keeping writes inside .bober/evolve/ makes Phase 5 fully reversible.

resolvedClarifications.answer: A — files under .bober/replay/cases/*.json + SQLite replay.db via the FactStore adapter pattern.
A — pure guard after runEvaluation, gated by config.selfImprove.deterministicGate (default false).
A — pure redactRubric stripping successCriteria/evaluatorNotes/verificationMethod, gated by config.selfImprove.rubricIsolation (default false).
A — downgrade uncited FAIL details to passed:true info, gated by config.selfImprove.requireCitedArtifact (default false).
A — evolve writes only under .bober/evolve/, never to agents/ and never into runPipeline.

assumptions: Replay fixtures live at .bober/replay/cases/<caseId>.json (immutable) with a SQLite baseline index .bober/replay/replay.db, built with the FactStore adapter pattern (src/state/facts.ts:136): deterministic content-hash id, timestamps injected as params, :memory:-testable, CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS in ctor.

All new behavior is gated by a new config.selfImprove section (Zod, added to BoberConfigSchema as optional, every flag default false), mirroring EvaluatorSectionSchema (src/config/schema.ts:104). With the section absent or all-false, runPipeline / runEvaluatorAgent behavior is byte-identical to today.

The deterministic-first gate is inserted in runEvaluatorAgent after runEvaluation (src/orchestrator/evaluator-agent.ts:81) as a pure predicate over the programmatic results' passed+required fields; it skips runAgentEvaluation only when a required strategy failed AND the flag is on.

Rubric isolation is a pure redactRubric(handoff) applied at createHandoff in pipeline.ts (:276); the generator keeps title/description/definitionOfDone/generatorNotes/nonGoals and loses successCriteria/evaluatorNotes/verificationMethod.

The cite-artifact guard is a pure fn over EvalResult.details (src/contracts/eval-result.ts:10) that downgrades uncited FAIL details; an EvalDetail is cited if it has a file (optionally line) or a failing-test-name / command-output substring in message.

GEPA reads/writes agent prompt text via the agents/<role>.md contract (src/orchestrator/agent-loader.ts loadAgentDefinition: body after frontmatter is the systemPrompt); evolve writes only under .bober/evolve/<runId>/, scores variants ONLY via runReplayHarness, and never writes agents/ or touches runPipeline.

GEPA mutation operators are a small deterministic-seeded set (paraphrase / add-constraint / reorder); the precise operator choice is the generator's call — the replay gate, not the operator, is the load-bearing constraint.

CLI verbs replay and evolve follow the register<X>Command(program) pattern (src/cli/commands/facts.ts), resolve root via findProjectRoot, use chalk, and never throw (set process.exitCode=1 and return).

Runs identically on Anthropic or DeepSeek: replay/guards do no LLM calls except GEPA variant generation, which goes through the existing provider-agnostic createClient (src/providers/factory.ts) — no SDK import outside providers/.
outOfScope: Auto-promoting an evolved prompt into agents/<role>.md (writes stay under .bober/evolve/; promotion is a manual human copy)
Wiring any new gate or verb into runPipeline by default (all flags off-by-default; deterministicGate/rubricIsolation/requireCitedArtifact are opt-in)
GEPA on-line / in-the-loop evolution during a live run (offline CLI only)
Evolving lesson text or semantic_facts (Phase 5 targets generator/evaluator prompt instruction text only)
Re-running the actual generator LLM during replay (replay re-derives verdicts from captured programmatic strategies + frozen diff digests, not by re-generating code)
A vector/semantic similarity layer over the replay corpus (deterministic exact comparison only)

features.title: Replay regression corpus + store
features.description: An immutable golden corpus of past (contract, diff digest, eval verdict) tuples under .bober/replay/, with a pure SQLite-backed baseline index and a capture/list/show CLI, plus a new off-by-default selfImprove config section.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: A ReplayStore adapter persists immutable cases to .bober/replay/cases/*.json + a baseline index .bober/replay/replay.db, with deterministic caseIds and injected timestamps, testable against an in-memory DB.
AC2: `bober replay capture` ingests existing .bober/eval-results/*.json into the corpus; `replay list` and `replay show <id>` print the corpus and per-case provenance; handlers never throw.

features.title: Deterministic replay gate
features.description: A pure comparator and a `bober replay run` verb that re-derives each case's verdict deterministically, diffs vs the recorded baseline, reports pass/fail deltas, and exits non-zero on any regression. Exposes runReplayHarness() for GEPA to import as its gate.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober replay run` prints a per-case delta table and sets exit code 1 iff at least one case regressed vs baseline (exit 0 with a no-regression message otherwise).
AC2: A public runReplayHarness(projectRoot, config) returns {regressions, improvements, unchanged} for programmatic reuse by the evolve verb.

features.title: Evaluator anti-degeneration guards
features.description: Three pure, off-by-default guards on the generator↔evaluator loop: deterministic-first short-circuit of the LLM judge, rubric isolation from the generator prompt, and rejection of uncited FAIL details.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When config.selfImprove.deterministicGate is true and a required programmatic strategy failed, runEvaluatorAgent returns a FAIL without invoking runAgentEvaluation; with the flag false the LLM judge still runs (byte-identical to today).

AC2: When rubricIsolation is true the generator-bound handoff omits successCriteria/evaluatorNotes; when requireCitedArtifact is true an uncited FAIL detail is downgraded so it cannot fail the sprint; both default false and are no-ops when unset.

features.title: GEPA offline prompt evolution
features.description: An offline `bober evolve` verb that mutates generator/evaluator prompt variants, keeps a Pareto set, scores variants ONLY against the replay harness, and writes a promoted prompt under .bober/evolve/ only if it beats the baseline with zero regressions — never writing agents/ and never invoked by runPipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober evolve --role generator|evaluator` reads agents/<role>.md, generates variants, scores each via runReplayHarness, and writes the winner to .bober/evolve/<runId>/promoted/<role>.md only when it strictly beats baseline with zero regressions.
AC2: No code path in the evolve feature writes to agents/ and evolve is not referenced by runPipeline; a regressing or non-improving variant produces a 'no promotion' report and no promoted/ file.

techNotes.dataModel: ReplayCase {caseId(hash), contractId, iteration, baselineVerdict(pass|fail), diffDigest, evalDetails[]} persisted as .bober/replay/cases/<caseId>.json + a baseline row in .bober/replay/replay.db. GEPA variants under .bober/evolve/<runId>/ with a report.json Pareto record.
securityConsiderations: GEPA must never write to agents/ or the live pipeline — enforced by tests that grep for forbidden write targets
All new flags default off so an accidental config omission cannot change live behavior
No SDK import outside providers/; GEPA variant generation goes through the provider-agnostic createClient

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: With config.selfImprove absent or all flags false, runPipeline and runEvaluatorAgent are byte-identical to pre-Phase-5 behavior.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: A vitest invariant test asserts handoff and evaluation outputs are unchanged when all selfImprove flags are false.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: TypeScript strict zero errors and tsc clean build across all new modules.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: npm run typecheck and npm run build exit 0.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: GEPA never writes to agents/ and is never invoked by runPipeline.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: A vitest test greps the evolve source + runPipeline for any write to agents/ or call to evolve and asserts none exist.

constraints: ESM everywhere (.js import specifiers, NodeNext); import type syntax; _-prefixed unused params
Zod for all config validation; new selfImprove section is optional with every flag .default(false)
Filesystem state in .bober/ (JSON files); SQLite only via the existing better-sqlite3 adapter pattern
Provider-agnostic: no @anthropic-ai/sdk or openai import outside providers/; runs on Anthropic or DeepSeek
Additive + reversible: no live pipeline behavior change by default; every new gate/verb opt-in or off-by-default
